name: Finland (state flag, 1917–1918)

colors.symbolism: the golden lion of the arms
the lion's armour and sword and the nine heraldic roses

description: A red field bearing the golden lion of the Finnish coat of arms — a crowned lion brandishing a sword, its raised forepaw an armoured arm, trampling a sabre, surrounded by nine white heraldic roses.
history: Finland's semi-official first state flag: a red field charged with the golden lion of the national arms, flown from December 1917. A majority in Parliament considered the red ground unsuitable, and after the Civil War the blue-cross-on-white design was adopted by the flag act of 29 May 1918.

features.role: arms of Finland — a crowned golden lion with sword, amid nine roses
